parts.text: Patient presents with chest pain, heart palpitations, shortness of breath, dizziness, and sweating episodes over the past 3 months. Episodes last 10-20 minutes and occur without warning, typically once or twice a week. Patient reports feeling of impending doom during episodes and fears having a heart attack. Medical history includes anxiety disorder diagnosed at age 25, managed with CBT and occasional benzodiazepines. GERD diagnosed at age 27, managed with PPIs and dietary changes. High-stress job as investment banker, occasional caffeine and alcohol use, irregular exercise. Current medications: Lorazepam 0.5mg as needed for anxiety, Omeprazole 20mg daily. Recent ECG shows normal sinus rhythm, no ischemia or arrhythmia. Cardiac enzymes normal, thyroid function normal. Holter monitor shows no significant arrhythmias, occasional PVCs. Echocardiogram shows normal cardiac structure and function, ejection fraction 60%. Vital signs: BP 122/78 mmHg, HR 82 bpm, BMI 23.4. Cardiovascular exam normal, respiratory exam clear. Please provide comprehensive medical diagnosis analysis.
